subject: Astronomy & Space
title: Reionization: Lighting Up the Cosmos
description: Learn Reionization: Lighting Up the Cosmos in one minute.

hook.text: After the Dark Ages ended, the first stars and galaxies flooded space with ultraviolet radiation.

buildup.text: This radiation stripped electrons from hydrogen atoms—re-ionizing the gas that had been neutral for millions of years.

discovery.text: Reionization turned the opaque universe transparent again. Light could travel vast distances without being absorbed.

twist.text: The process wasn't instant. It took hundreds of millions of years, with ionized bubbles slowly merging together.

climax.text: Studying reionization tells us when and how the first luminous objects turned on—the universe's cosmic dawn.

punchline.text: Stars lit up, gas cleared, and the universe became see-through again.

quiz.question: What caused reionization of the universe?
options: Ultraviolet radiation from the first stars and galaxies stripped electrons from hydrogen
A second Big Bang event released energy that ionized all matter
Dark energy heated the neutral gas until it ionized
